  • People
  • Alpaslan Duysak

Alpaslan Duysak

  • Instructional Associate Professor, Computer Science & Engineering
  • Phone: 409-740-5513
  • Email: aduysak03@tamu.edu
  • Office: ECRD 309G (Galveston)
Google Scholar Profile

Educational Background

  • Ph.D. National Centre for Computer Animation, Bournemouth University – 2004
  • M.S. Electrical Engineering, The Pennsylvania State University – 1997
  • B.S. Electrical-Electronics Engineering, Gazi University – 1992

Research Interests

    • Computer graphics
    • Deformation algorithms
    • Haptic design
    • Machine learning
    • Optimization

Awards & Honors

  • Research Assistantship, Bournemouth University – 1999
  • Turkish Higher Education Bursary – 1993

Selected Publications

  • Duysak, A., et al. "Cross grouping strategy based 2DPCA method for face recognition." Applied Soft Computing, 29, 270-279, 2015.
  • Duysak, et al. "The modelling of interactions between organs and medical tools: a volumetric mass-spring chain algorithm." Computer Methods in Biomechanics and Biomedical Engineering. 17(5), 488-496, 2014.
  • Duysak, A., et al. "Molecular Modeling for Deformable Object Simulation." International Conference on Computer Graphics and Virtual Reality, pp. 28-31, July 13-16, 2009.
  • Duysak, A. "Triangle Propagation for Mass-Spring Chain Algorithm," ISCIS 2006, Lecture Notes in Computer Science (LNCS), 4263, pp. 306–315, 2006.
  • Duysak, A, et at. "Efficient Modeling and Simulation of Soft Tissue Deformation Using Mass-Spring Systems," The 17th International Congress and Exhibition on Computer Assisted Radiology and Surgery (CARS, London), pp. 337-342, 2003.
Google Scholar Profile
